pi_items.source: setup_task.sh and export_result.sh explicitly target this exact output path.
setup_task.sh injects Length 3.0 m into the nacelle integration spec text file.
setup_task.sh injects Maximum Diameter 1.6 m into the nacelle integration spec text file.
setup_task.sh injects X 7.5 m into the specification document.
setup_task.sh injects Y 5.0 m into the specification document.
setup_task.sh injects Z -1.2 m into the specification document.
export_result.sh writes the extracted metadata to this specific JSON file.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on the real NASA eCRM-001 conceptual aircraft model (eCRM-001_wing_tail.vsp3), while the specific nacelle integration specifications (length 3.0, diameter 1.6, position X=7.5, Y=5.0, Z=-1.2) are synthetically generated and explicitly injected via setup_task.sh. It has been verified that all output paths and dimensional targets in the metadata perfectly match the setup instructions and verifier evaluation constraints. However, a critical defect was discovered in the verifier.py script: it explicitly parses the VSP3 file using a regular expression that searches for <Component> and <Type> XML tags. Because OpenVSP 3 files utilize <Geom> and <TypeName> structures instead, this logic perpetually fails to find any components in the exported model. Consequently, the task will unconditionally fail with the message No new nacelle/pod component found in the model, rendering it programmatically unscorable even if the agent completes the work perfectly.
